intent_questions.question: What behavior must be proven?
intent_questions.self_answer: GET / from the README Flask app should return HTTP 200 and the exact body Hello, World!.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may test a different route, add HTML wrappers, or skip the body contract.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Replace the route and body only with exact public repository evidence.

intent_questions.question: What is the system boundary: Runner target, Mocker dependency, hook host, or configuration-as-code?
intent_questions.self_answer: The Runner target is an externally-started Flask HTTP process on 127.0.0.1:5000; QaaS YAML does not start or clean that process.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: Process lifecycle could be incorrectly encoded as undocumented QaaS YAML.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide documented QaaS lifecycle support or keep startup in an external harness plan.

intent_questions.question: What public docs/schema path proves the capability exists?
intent_questions.self_answer: QaaS public docs/schema support HTTP Transactions, FromFileSystem data-source payload bytes, HttpStatus assertions, and custom assertions by short type name; the ExactHttpTextBody hook is authored but not schema/template/live validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may invent a built-in text body assertion or treat sidecar hook code as executable validation.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide template/build/live validation for the custom assertion packet.

intent_questions.question: What inputs, outputs, and side effects prove success?
intent_questions.self_answer: Input is a GET request to /. Outputs are HTTP status 200 and body Hello, World!. Side effects should be read-only for this GET.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may over-assert mutations, persistence, or unrelated endpoints.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide public side-effect evidence for any additional assertion.

intent_questions.question: Which negative, malformed, outage, retry, cleanup, and observability cases matter?
intent_questions.self_answer: Negative, malformed, retry, and observability cases remain blocked; this candidate covers only one README-backed happy path and cleanup planning.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may claim broad framework or application coverage from one happy-path route.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Add exact public negative-case and observability contracts before expanding assertions.

intent_questions.question: Which dependencies must exist: broker, HTTP endpoint, database, Redis, S3, Kubernetes, filesystem, credentials, ports?
intent_questions.self_answer: Dependencies are Python, Flask CLI/runtime access, app.py in the runtime working directory, and port 5000 availability; those dependencies are identified from public evidence but not live-validated.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may be promoted where Python, Flask, port, or fixture placement is not available.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Provide validated dependency transcripts for the target environment.

intent_questions.question: What can run now, and what must be deferred?
intent_questions.self_answer: Static candidate validation can run now. Process lifecycle, custom assertion build/schema/template validation, live act/assert execution, cleanup proof, and live weak-model validation are deferred and block promotion.
intent_questions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may confuse a candidate packet with an executable test.
intent_questions.how_to_override: Attach passing lifecycle, custom assertion build/schema/template, live, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence.

intent_assumptions.assumption: The README simple example is the public behavior contract for this candidate.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: It contains exact app.py code, command, URL, and body markers harvested from an immutable Git blob.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: The candidate may not match another Flask app or route.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Replace the selected contract with another immutable public blob.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Process lifecycle belongs to the external harness until QaaS docs prove otherwise.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The public QaaS Runner docs describe HTTP transactions but not starting arbitrary Python processes.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: Startup or cleanup may be missed if a future QaaS lifecycle feature exists.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide public QaaS lifecycle docs and update the checker.

intent_assumptions.assumption: Plain-text body comparison is represented by a custom assertion sidecar, but remains blocked until build/schema/template/live validation passes.
intent_assumptions.why_safe: The available built-ins do not prove exact raw text equality, while public custom assertion docs support sidecar authoring by short type name.
intent_assumptions.risk_if_wrong: A weak model may treat authored code as runtime validation.
intent_assumptions.how_to_override: Provide passing custom assertion build, schema, template, and live QaaS evidence.

cases.scenario: Call README-backed Flask GET / and assert documented HTTP success while the sidecar exact text-body assertion remains build/schema/template/live blocked.
setup: Start Flask externally with the README app.py using the README command.
Use get-root.bin as the byte payload driver for the GET transaction.
action: Run QaaS Runner against test.qaas.yaml after template validation is available.
assertions: Exact body Hello, World! is preserved as expected-response evidence but blocked from YAML until a documented text assertion or hook is selected.
cleanup: Terminate the tracked Flask process tree.
Remove candidate session-data after live validation.
cases.blocked_reason: QaaS template, live act/assert, exact plain-text body assertion build/schema/template validation, cleanup, and live weak-model validation have not passed.

cases.scenario: Keep Python process startup and cleanup outside QaaS YAML until public QaaS docs prove lifecycle support.
setup: Use candidate-runtime-plan.json as an external harness plan only.
action: Start and stop Flask through a tracked process runner, not through undocumented QaaS YAML fields.
assertions: Runtime plan remains blocked and never counts as executable promotion evidence by itself.
cleanup: Tracked process tree termination must be validated before promotion.
cases.blocked_reason: Process lifecycle and cleanup are planned but not validated.

cases.scenario: Tie every candidate field to immutable selected public contract evidence and QaaS public docs.
setup: Review selected-contract.json, README blob, and QaaS schema docs.
assertions: Candidate stays blocked until executable evidence exists.
No undocumented QaaS fields are introduced.
cleanup: No external resources are created by static candidate validation.
cases.blocked_reason: Static evidence does not prove live behavior.

assertions: Candidate Runner YAML uses schema-derived HttpStatus StatusCode/OutputNames.
Candidate preserves README-backed plain-text body evidence and includes a sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion packet that is not wired into active YAML before validation.
Candidate remains blocked until lifecycle, template, live QaaS, cleanup, airgapped, and strong-review evidence pass.

configuration_contract.OutputName: Required transaction output name to inspect.
configuration_contract.ExpectedText: Required exact expected response body text.
configuration_contract.EncodingName: Optional encoding name; defaults to utf-8.

builtins_considered.name: HttpStatus
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Status-only assertion; does not compare response body bytes.

builtins_considered.name: OutputContentByExpectedCsvResults
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: JSON/CSV field mapping assertion; not exact raw/plain-text body equality.

builtins_considered.name: OutputDeserializableTo
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Checks deserialization success, not equality with exact public README text.

builtins_considered.name: ObjectOutputJsonSchema
builtins_considered.reason_insufficient: Checks JSON schema compatibility, not exact raw/plain-text body equality.

dependency_gates.blocked_reason: Live weak-model validation has not passed.

required_evidence: Public API, CLI, or runtime contract
Documented QaaS body assertion or custom hook for plain text
C# build result when code artifacts exist
Live QaaS run/act/assert result when dependency gates are ready

cleanup: No live cleanup was performed during static candidate generation.
validation_sequence: Validate selected immutable public contract evidence.
Build and schema/template-validate the sidecar ExactHttpTextBody custom assertion before executable promotion.
Run QaaS template validation when a Runner host is available.
Run live QaaS act/assert against tracked Flask process.
Run live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review before promotion.

source_only_blockers.description: Live weak-model validation has not passed for this candidate packet.
required_evidence: Airgapped transcript with dry_run false and expected output contract preserved
source_only_blockers.unblock_instruction: Run D:\QaaS\_tools\weak-model-session.ps1 -Airgapped -All -ReasoningEffort none when quota/routing is available.

blocked_reason: Selected Flask candidate passed lifecycle, custom ExactHttpTextBody build/template/live validation, and QaaS template/live validation, but executable promotion remains blocked until live airgapped weak-model validation and strong review pass.

validation_advisories.description: The quick-start example uses stale HttpStatus field names, while generated schema docs require StatusCode and OutputNames.
validation_advisories.resolved_by: schema-derived StatusCode/OutputNames template and live validation
